mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling, the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows declines and risk appetite improves, so S&P 500 returns over the next month are positive.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are not positive in a majority of months when FEDFUNDS is declining versus the prior month, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals slowing labor income growth and weaker demand, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations generate positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 60% of the time, or if the signal's out-of-sample Rank IC is not negative over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST and RSAFS both accelerate together, housing activity and retail demand confirm broad final-demand strength, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-signal months do not outperform unconditional 60-day returns by at least 1 percentage point on average, or if one of the two series alone fully explains the effect.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO indicates improving industrial output and stronger cyclical earnings momentum, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Reject if weekly forward returns are not positive more often than not after INDPRO month-over-month increases, or if the effect vanishes in every rolling 18-month subsample.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is elevated while FEDFUNDS remains below inflation, real rates are deeply negative and equities reprice upward as discounting pressure eases, generating positive returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS by more than 1.0 percentage point
failure_condition: Reject if the spread-conditioned sample has negative average 20-day returns, or if the interaction term adds no predictive power beyond either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in PAYEMS signals payroll contraction and lower household income growth, which weakens revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive-payroll-decline episodes do not precede negative 60-day returns on average, or if the hit rate is not worse than 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in GDPC1 and RSAFS marks broad demand deceleration, so earnings revisions turn lower and S&P 500 returns over the next month are negative.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and RSAFS both decelerate versus their prior released values
failure_condition: Reject if the joint slowdown regime does not produce lower 20-day returns than either slowdown alone, or if average returns are not negative in the triggered sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a policy-tightening regime, a rising FEDFUNDS rate combined with sticky CPIAUCSL inflation signals more restrictive financial conditions, which should push S&P 500 returns negative over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint tightening-inflation condition is followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the signal ceases to be negative once rate hikes are excluded.
